title: Has Big Ben ever gone wrong?

meanings.usage: 用于描述广为人知的人或事物
用于表示建造或竖立建筑、雕像等
be responsible for表示对某事负责
用于形容尺寸或规模非常大
用于描述正确无误的测量或信息
用于指政府或机构的工作人员
用于指观察天体或天气的地方
用于覆盖表面的液体材料
hang的过去式为hung，表示悬挂某物
burn down表示建筑等被烧毁

examples.en: Big Ben takes its name from Sir Benjamin Hall.
examples.zh: 大本钟以本杰明·霍尔爵士的名字命名。

examples.en: He was responsible for the making of the clock.
It is not only of immense size, but is extremely accurate as well.
examples.zh: 它不仅体积巨大，而且非常精确。

examples.en: Microphones are connected to the clock tower.
He hung a pot of paint on one of the hands.
examples.zh: 他把一罐油漆挂在指针上。

examples.en: The Houses of Parliament had been burned down in 1834.
examples.zh: 议会大厦在1834年被烧毁了。

examples.en: Officials have the clock checked twice a day.
examples.zh: 官员们每天两次检查这个钟。

examples.en: The clock can be heard all over the world.
examples.zh: 这个钟的声音全世界都能听到。

examples.en: It failed to give the correct time.
examples.zh: 它没能报出正确的时间。

grammar.title: 现在完成时 (Present Perfect Tense)
grammar.definition: 表示过去发生的动作或状态持续到现在，或对现在造成的影响。常与already, yet, ever, never, just等时间状语连用。
grammar.structure: 主语 + have/has + 过去分词
grammar.usage: 用于询问某事是否曾经发生过，强调与现在的联系

examples.en: Has Big Ben ever gone wrong?
examples.zh: 大本钟曾经出过故障吗？

grammar.title: 与现在事实相反的虚拟语气 (Conditional 2)
grammar.definition: 表示与现在事实相反或不太可能发生的假设。主句用would + 动词原形，从句用过去式(be用were)
grammar.structure: If + 主语 + 过去式..., 主语 + would + 动词原形...
grammar.usage: 用于表达与事实相反的假设情况

examples.en: If the Houses of Parliament had not been burned down, the great clock would never have been erected.
examples.zh: 如果议会大厦没有被烧毁，大本钟就永远不会建成。

grammar.title: 被动语态 (Passive Voice)
grammar.definition: 强调动作的承受者而非执行者，由be动词 + 过去分词构成
grammar.structure: 主语 + be + 过去分词 (+ by + 动作执行者)
grammar.usage: 当动作的承受者比执行者更重要时使用

examples.en: The great clock would never have been erected.
examples.zh: 大本钟就永远不会建成了。

examples.en: The clock is checked twice a day.
examples.zh: 这个钟每天被检查两次。

sentencePatterns.pattern: Has + 主语 + ever + 过去分词?

original.en: Has Big Ben ever gone wrong?
original.zh: 大本钟曾经出过故障吗？

imitations.en: Has she ever been to London?
Has he ever made a mistake?

sentencePatterns.pattern: If + 主语 + had not + 过去分词..., 主语 + would never have + 过去分词

original.en: If the Houses of Parliament had not been burned down, the great clock would never have been erected.
original.zh: 如果议会大厦没有被烧毁，大本钟就永远不会建成。

imitations.en: If I had not studied hard, I would never have passed the exam.
imitations.zh: 如果我没有努力学习，我就不会通过考试。

imitations.en: If it had not rained, we would never have stayed at home.
imitations.zh: 如果不下雨，我们就不会待在家里。

sentencePatterns.pattern: 主语 + is not only...but + be动词 + 形容词 as well

original.en: It is not only of immense size, but is extremely accurate as well.
original.zh: 它不仅体积巨大，而且非常精确。

imitations.en: She is not only beautiful but kind as well.
This book is not only interesting but useful as well.
imitations.zh: 这本书不仅有趣，而且有用。

sentencePatterns.pattern: A + 名词 + who/which/that + 过去分词...

original.en: A painter who had been working on the tower hung a pot of paint on one of the hands.
original.zh: 一个一直在塔上工作的油漆匠把一罐油漆挂在指针上。

imitations.en: A student who was reading in the library lost his book.
imitations.zh: 一个在图书馆读书的学生丢了书。

imitations.en: The man who was waiting for the bus was very cold.
imitations.zh: 那个等公交车的人非常冷。
